info.title: MotoDiag API
info.description: Motorcycle diagnostic + shop management API. Exposes the Track G shop console + Track A-F diagnostic substrate over HTTP.

get.description:
Returns 200 when the DB responds; 503 otherwise.

Used by load balancers / container schedulers. Does not exercise
any downstream repos — minimal work per probe.

Client contract check.

Mobile clients (Track I) call this on startup to detect schema
drift and refuse to write against an incompatible server.

get.summary: Fetch a shop profile by id
get.description:
Return the shop row, or 404 via
:class:`ShopNotFoundError` (mapped by the global exception
handler to an RFC 7807 ProblemDetail).

schema.title: Response Get Shop Endpoint V1 Shops  Shop Id  Get

post.description:
Returns a checkout URL. Client redirects user to this URL to
complete payment. On success, Stripe sends
``customer.subscription.created`` to the webhook endpoint and
the subscription row is populated.

post.summary: Get a Stripe Customer Portal URL
post.description:
Generate a temporary portal URL where the user can update
their payment method, download invoices, or cancel.

get.summary: List vehicles in the caller's garage

post.summary: Add a vehicle to the caller's garage

get.summary: Fetch one vehicle from the caller's garage

delete.summary: Hard-delete a vehicle (no FK refs allowed)

Append a note to a session

List shops the caller has a membership at

ProblemDetail.title: ProblemDetail
ProblemDetail.description: RFC 7807 Problem Details body. All error responses use this shape.

type.description: URI reference identifying the problem type.

instance.description: URI reference for the specific problem.

request_id.description: Server-issued correlation id; matches the `X-Request-ID` response header.

apiKey.name: X-API-Key
apiKey.description: API key issued via `POST /v1/billing/checkout-session` or the `motodiag apikey create` CLI. Prefix `mdk_live_...` for production or `mdk_test_...` for dev/test. Plaintext is shown ONCE at creation time; keep it safe.

bearerAuth.description: Same key as `apiKey`, supplied as `Authorization: Bearer <key>` instead of the dedicated header. Useful for OpenAPI clients that only support HTTP Bearer.

Unauthorized.description: Missing, malformed, or revoked API key. Supply a valid `X-API-Key` header or `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

SubscriptionRequired.description: Caller is authenticated but lacks the required subscription tier. Upgrade via `POST /v1/billing/checkout-session` or the Stripe customer portal.

Forbidden.description: Caller is authenticated and has the right tier, but lacks access to this specific resource (e.g. cross-shop request).

NotFound.description: Resource does not exist OR belongs to another owner — both cases return 404 to avoid enumeration attacks.

ValidationError.description: Request body failed Pydantic validation. Response body's `detail` lists the invalid fields.

RateLimitExceeded.description: Per-minute or per-day rate limit for the caller's tier has been exceeded. `Retry-After` header indicates wait-time in seconds.

X-RateLimit-Tier.description: Tier bucket used for this request (anonymous / individual / shop / company).

InternalError.description: Unexpected server error. `detail` is intentionally omitted on 5xx responses — check the correlation `request_id` and server logs.

tags.name: meta
tags.description: Health checks and version metadata. No authentication required; used by load balancers and client startup probes.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: API keys, subscriptions, and Stripe webhooks. This is the entry point for the paywall — every non-meta endpoint requires an active API key.

tags.name: shops
tags.description: Global shop registry. Read-only at this tier; see `shop-management` for writes.

tags.name: billing
tags.description: Stripe checkout sessions + customer portals + webhook intake. Prices are set per subscription tier.

tags.name: vehicles
tags.description: Caller's garage — add, list, update, or remove bikes you own. Tier-quota gated (individual=5, shop=50, company=unlimited).

tags.name: sessions
tags.description: Diagnostic session lifecycle: open a session, record symptoms + fault codes, save the AI diagnosis, close the session. Monthly-quota gated.

tags.name: knowledge-base
tags.description: Read-only lookups: DTC codes, symptoms, known issues, and a unified search across all three. Available to any authenticated caller (no tier required).

tags.name: shop-management
tags.description: The full shop-operator console: members, customers, work orders, issues, parts, invoices, analytics. Shop-tier required + per-shop membership enforced.

tags.name: reports
tags.description: Downloadable reports in JSON or PDF — diagnostic session reports (session owner), work-order receipts (shop-tier), invoice PDFs (shop-tier).

tags.name: live
tags.description: WebSocket live sensor-data streams keyed to a diagnostic session. Paid-tier required.
